title: Sun: Point light intensity does not follow inverse-square law — equal brightness at Mercury and Pluto
description:
The Sun's PointLight in src/objects/Sun.js (line 58) is constructed as:

  new THREE.PointLight(0xFFEEBB, 3.5, 15000)

Three problems:

1. No decay parameter — Three.js PointLight decay defaults differ by version; without an explicit decay=2, the light may use linear (decay=1) or flat (decay=0) falloff instead of the physically correct inverse-square law. All planets then receive nearly identical illumination regardless of orbital distance.

2. Intensity is uncalibrated for AU scale — the scene uses AU_SCALE=100 (1 AU = 100 Three.js units). A G2V star at that scale should have intensity proportional to luminosity × AU_SCALE², so that the solar constant at Earth (1 AU = 100 units) gives a reference illuminance of 1.0. The current value of 3.5 is arbitrary and produces no meaningful flux gradient across the solar system.

3. Distance cap of 15000 is unnecessary and interacts badly with decay — with proper inverse-square decay (decay=2, distance=0 means infinite range), the falloff is handled naturally. The cap causes a hard cutoff rather than a smooth rolloff and can interfere with the attenuation curve for mid-range planets.

Expected physical behavior (G2V star, AU_SCALE=100):
  Mercury (0.387 AU = 38.7 units): receives ~6.7× Earth flux
  Earth   (1.0  AU = 100  units): reference (1.0 solar constant)
  Mars    (1.52 AU = 152  units): receives ~0.43× Earth flux
  Jupiter (5.2  AU = 520  units): receives ~0.037× Earth flux
  Neptune (30   AU = 3000 units): receives ~0.001× Earth flux

Fix:
  this.light = new THREE.PointLight(0xFFEEBB, /* intensity */ 10000, /* distance */ 0, /* decay */ 2)

Where intensity=10000 is derived from: reference_illuminance × (Earth_distance_units)² = 1.0 × 100² = 10000. The decay=2 enforces inverse-square law. distance=0 means no artificial cap (natural falloff only). Tune the exact intensity value so Mercury surfaces are visibly brighter than Neptunian ones, matching a G2V spectral luminosity class.

title: Infra: Physics engine test suite — orbital position accuracy, rigid body sim
description: Unit tests for OrbitalMechanicsWorld: verify planet positions against JPL Horizons data (±0.1% AU). Integration tests for rigid body: sphere drop, stack stability. Particle tests: emitter spawn rate, lifetime. All tests headless (no canvas). Run in Node.js via Vitest.

title: Infra: DSL transpiler test suite — lexer, parser, codegen unit tests
description: Vitest-based unit tests for: lexer (token stream assertions for 50+ DSL snippets), parser (AST shape assertions), semantic analysis (error cases: undefined symbols, type mismatches), codegen (golden-file JS output for canonical DSL programs). Target: >90% line coverage on transpiler.

title: StdLib: artlab/animation — keyframe timeline, tween, audio-reactive bindings
description: DSL animation library: timeline { at 0s { camera.position: (100,80,400), ease: power2 }, at 10s { ... } }. tween { target: sun.scale, from: 1.0, to: 1.2, duration: 0.3s, ease: elastic }. Reactive: bind { source: fft.bass, target: bloom.strength, range: (1.0, 2.5) }.

title: StdLib: artlab/cameras — orbit, fly, path-follow, cinematic timeline camera
description: DSL camera library: 'camera orbit { fov: 55, near: 0.1, far: 200000 }', 'camera path { ... keyframes ... }'. Orbit camera wraps OrbitControls with damping config from DSL. Cinematic path camera takes a DSL timeline of position/lookAt pairs with easing.

title: StdLib: artlab/physics/orbital — Kepler orbit DSL wrappers and solar constants
description: DSL orbital library: 'use artlab/physics/orbital'. Provides: orbit { sma: 1.0 AU, ecc: 0.017, inc: 0.0 deg, period: 365.25 d } and keplerPosition(), solveKepler(). Solar system constants: AU_SCALE, PLANET_SCALE, solar masses. The solar system demo uses only this library for all orbital motion.

title: StdLib: artlab/geometry — sphere, box, cylinder, torus, plane, custom mesh
description: DSL geometry library: 'use artlab/geometry'. Provides: sphere(radius, segments), box(w,h,d), cylinder(r,h,segs), torus(r,tube,segs), plane(w,h), ring(inner,outer), icosphere(subdivisions). Each emits a Three.js BufferGeometry via generated code. Parameters DSL-typed.

title: StdLib: artlab/lights — ambient, point, directional, spot, hemisphere
description: DSL lights library: 'light point { color: #FFE8BB, intensity: 3.5, distance: 15000 }', 'light ambient { color: #0a1020, intensity: 0.08 }', etc. Emits Three.js light objects. Supports shadow map configuration per-light.

title: StdLib: Standard Artlab DSL libraries — math, geometry, physics, audio, UI, animation
description: All standard libraries written in the Artlab DSL itself (with JS runtime backing where needed). Loadable via 'use artlab/...' import. Included as embedded packages in the Artlab distribution.

title: StdLib: artlab/math — Vec2/3/4, Mat3/4, Quat, easing, noise, units
description: DSL math library: vector and matrix types with operator overloads (dot, cross, normalize, lerp). Quaternion construction and slerp. Easing functions (linear, cubic, elastic, bounce). Perlin/simplex noise. Unit conversions (AU, km, m; degrees/radians; earth-masses, kg). All usable in DSL expressions.

title: Audio: DSL bindings — declare audio sources, FFT bindings, synth in DSL
description: Extend DSL codegen to emit audio setup. Syntax: 'audio background { src: "music.ogg", loop: true }', 'audio spatial { src: "rumble.wav", node: sun }', 'on audio:beat(bass) { animate sun.scale by 1.1 over 0.1s }'. FFT band values exposed as reactive DSL variables usable in animation and physics force expressions.

title: Audio: FFT pipeline — band analysis, beat detection, normalized data struct
description: FFTPipeline: connects an analyser node to the audio graph. Each frame extracts frequency and time-domain data. Computes: bass (20-250Hz), mid (250-4kHz), high (4k-20kHz) RMS levels (0-1 normalized). Beat detection: onset strength + peak-picking per band. Fires audio:beat events on EventBus. Exposes fft.data for animation bindings.

title: Audio: AudioEngine — Web Audio context, lazy start, master gain
description: AudioEngine: wraps AudioContext (lazy init on first user gesture). Master gain node. Play/pause/stop. Exposes connect(source) for mic input or AudioBuffer playback. Manages suspend/resume for background tabs.

title: Assets: Audio asset loader — mp3/ogg/wav from package zip to AudioBuffer
description: AudioAssetLoader: extracts audio file blob from zip, decodes via AudioContext.decodeAudioData(). Caches the AudioBuffer. Supports: background music (looping), one-shot SFX, and sample data for the synthesizer. Integrates with AudioEngine's asset registry.

title: Audio: Spatial audio, FFT analysis, synthesizer, DSL bindings
description: Generalize the current audio code into a standalone AudioEngine with spatial positioning, FFT-to-event pipeline, synthesizer/pad system, and DSL bindings.

title: Assets: Texture loader — from package zip, procedural fallback, mipmaps
description: TextureLoader: loads image blobs from package zip (JPEG, PNG, WebP, KTX2 for GPU-compressed). Applies: SRGBColorSpace, LinearMipmapLinearFilter, anisotropy=16. Procedural fallback: canvas noise texture in the asset's declared fallback color. Fallback swaps to real texture asynchronously when loaded.

title: Assets: Unified asset loading from packages — textures, models, audio
description: AssetManager loads all asset types from the package zip (or URLs in dev mode). Handles caching, async loading, procedural fallbacks, and hot-reload in dev mode.

title: Assets: AssetManager — registry, async load queue, LRU cache, progress events
description: AssetManager: register(path, type) to queue an asset. loadAll() returns a Promise that resolves when all queued assets are ready. LRU cache (configurable max MB). Fires progress events (loaded/total) for loading screens. Assets keyed by package-relative path (e.g. 'textures/earth/daymap.jpg').

title: Runtime: Time manager — fixed/variable dt, simulation speed, pause/resume
description: TimeManager: wall-clock delta, simulation time (scaled by timeScale), fixed physics timestep accumulator. Exposes: dt (variable), fixedDt (fixed, default 1/60), elapsed, timeScale (0=paused, 1=normal, 2=fast, <0=rewind). All physics and animation systems step via TimeManager, not raw requestAnimationFrame.

title: Runtime: Event / signal bus — typed events across systems
description: EventBus: typed pub/sub. Core events: scene:ready, scene:update(dt), scene:destroy, input:key(key, state), input:pointer(x,y,buttons), physics:collision(a,b), audio:beat(band,strength), package:loaded, package:error. DSL scenes subscribe via 'on event { ... }' blocks. Decouples all systems.

title: Runtime: Input manager — keyboard, mouse, touch, gamepad, normalized API
description: InputManager: normalizes keyboard (keydown/keyup with repeat suppression), mouse (position NDC, buttons, wheel), touch (multi-touch, pinch-zoom), and Gamepad API. Fires events on EventBus. Allows remapping in DSL: 'bind key:Space to action:jump'. Handles pointer lock for first-person scenes.

title: Runtime: Scene loader — instantiate scene graph from compiled DSL output
description: SceneLoader.load(compiledModule): calls the module's exported scene factory, which returns a scene descriptor object. Loader walks the descriptor, creates SceneNodes, attaches components, registers physics bodies, loads assets (via AssetManager), registers animations, and connects the event bus. Returns a live Scene instance.

title: Runtime: Animation system — keyframes, tweens, timeline, audio-reactive params
description: AnimationSystem: timelines composed of tracks. Each track targets a node property (position.x, material.emissiveIntensity, physics.force) over time. Easing functions. Audio-reactive tracks: bind a track value to an FFT band (bass, mid, high, arbitrary bin). GSAP-compatible easing names. Play, pause, seek, loop, reverse.

title: Runtime: Scene graph, loader, animation, event bus, input, time management
description: The Artlab runtime ties renderer, physics, and assets together. Scene graph (node tree, transforms), animation system, event/signal bus, input manager, and time manager.

title: Runtime: Scene graph — node tree, transform hierarchy, parent-child
description: SceneNode: id, name, transform (position/rotation/scale), children[], components[]. Supports local and world space transforms (lazy world matrix computation). Components: MeshComponent, LightComponent, PhysicsBodyComponent, AudioSourceComponent, CameraComponent. Node lifecycle: create, attach, detach, destroy.

title: Physics: DSL bindings — declare physics bodies and forces in Artlab DSL
description: Extend the DSL code generator to emit physics world setup calls. DSL syntax: 'body orbital { sma: 1.0 AU, ecc: 0.017, period: 365.25 d }', 'body rigid { shape: sphere(1.0), mass: 5.0 }', 'force gravity { g: 9.81 }', 'constraint hinge { bodyA: ..., bodyB: ..., axis: Y }'. Generated code calls IPhysicsWorld API.

title: Physics: Rigid body module via Rapier.js (WASM, collision shapes, joints)
description: RigidBodyWorld wrapping Rapier.js (rapier3d-compat). Support body types: dynamic, static, kinematic. Collision shapes: sphere, box, capsule, convex hull, trimesh. Joints: fixed, revolute, prismatic, spherical. Expose collision events to the event bus. Run in a shared SharedArrayBuffer+Atomics worker for non-blocking sim.

title: Physics: Particle system — emitters, forces, lifetime, GPU instancing
description: ParticleWorld: manages particle emitters. Each emitter has: spawn rate, initial velocity (cone/sphere/point), lifetime, size curve, color-over-lifetime, drag, gravity scale, attractors, vortex forces. Particles rendered via Three.js InstancedMesh for GPU efficiency. CPU simulation; optional GPU simulation via transform feedback.

title: Physics: Orbital mechanics module (Kepler solver, N-body, inclination)
description: Extract and generalize the current Kepler solver into a standalone OrbitalMechanicsWorld implementing IPhysicsWorld. Support: elliptic orbits (semi-major axis, eccentricity, inclination, argument of periapsis, RAAN), Newton-Raphson Kepler equation solver, N-body perturbations (optional, toggled by scene descriptor), moon sub-orbits. AU and SI unit modes.

title: Physics: General physics engine — orbital, rigid body, particles, fluids
description: Pluggable physics world abstraction. Orbital mechanics module (Kepler/N-body). Rigid body via Rapier.js (WASM). Particle system physics. Simplified SPH fluid. Debug renderer. DSL bindings for all physics types.

title: Physics: IPhysicsWorld interface — backend-agnostic physics contract
description: Define IPhysicsWorld: createBody(desc), removeBody(id), addForce(bodyId, vec), step(dt), getTransform(bodyId), setGravity(vec), onCollision(cb), dispose(). Also define body descriptor types: orbital, rigid, particle, fluid. Separates physics from rendering completely.

title: Renderer: Camera abstraction — perspective, ortho, orbit, path-follow
description: CameraController: wraps Three.js camera + OrbitControls or custom path animation. Modes: free-orbit (mouse/touch), cinematic path (GSAP timeline of positions/targets), first-person, top-down. Camera parameters (FOV, near, far, focal distance) set from DSL. Smooth transitions between modes.

title: Renderer: Material system — PBR, emissive, unlit, and custom shader materials
description: MaterialFactory: given a MaterialDescriptor (type, color, maps, emissive, roughness, metalness, custom vert/frag), produce the appropriate Three.js material. Support: PBR (MeshStandardMaterial), emissive, unlit (MeshBasicMaterial), and custom GLSL via ShaderMaterial. Materials are hot-reloadable in dev mode.

title: Renderer: Post-processing pipeline (bloom, tone-map, FXAA, vignette)
description: PostProcessingChain: accepts an ordered list of pass descriptors from DSL; builds the EffectComposer pipeline. Supported passes: UnrealBloom, FXAA, SSAO (basic), vignette, color grading (LUT). Each pass is parameterized and audio-reactive parameters can be patched per-frame from the audio engine.

title: Renderer: WebGL2 backend implementing IRenderer via Three.js WebGLRenderer
description: Implement WebGL2Renderer wrapping THREE.WebGLRenderer. Wire up: ACESFilmic tone mapping, shadow maps, output color space, pixel ratio cap. Expose internal scene/camera for direct Three.js object insertion during the transition period. This backend is the default for all Artlab packages.

title: Renderer: Pluggable rendering engine (WebGL2 primary, WebGPU secondary)
description: Abstract the rendering backend behind an IRenderer interface. Implement a robust WebGL2 backend. Include material system, shader pipeline, post-processing, camera abstraction, shadows, LOD, and instanced rendering. WebGPU backend is a separate story.

title: Renderer: IRenderer interface — backend-agnostic rendering contract
description: Define the IRenderer TypeScript interface: createScene(), setCamera(), addObject(desc), removeObject(id), render(dt), resize(w,h), setPostProcessing(chain), dispose(). All higher-level code (scene loader, IDE preview) talks only to IRenderer, never to Three.js directly.

title: Packages: Runtime package loader (load .zip → transpile → execute)
description: PackageLoader.load(blob): reads manifest, extracts DSL entry file, runs through transpiler (resolving deps from the zip), eval()s or dynamically imports the generated JS, and hands the resulting scene module to the Artlab runtime for execution.

title: Packages: Static linker — fetch all URL deps and embed into a single zip
description: Implement the StaticLinker that: reads an existing package or manifest; walks all 'use url:...' imports transitively; fetches each dep source; rewrites import statements to 'use embedded:name'; bundles everything into a new PackageWriter output. This is the 'cargo build --release' equivalent.

title: Packages: Browser-side zip writer (serialize package for download)
description: Implement a PackageWriter class that: accepts a manifest object, DSL source files (as strings), and asset blobs; produces a zip Blob; triggers a browser download. Used by the IDE's 'Export Package' button and the static linker.

title: Packages: Dev-mode URL dependency resolver
description: In dev mode, when the transpiler encounters 'use url:https://...', fetch the DSL source from that URL (CORS-safe, with a local proxy fallback). Cache fetched sources in sessionStorage keyed by URL+ETag. Expose a DependencyResolver interface that the transpiler calls synchronously after an initial async prefetch pass.

title: Packages: Package manifest format specification (artlab.json)
description: Define the artlab.json manifest schema: name, version, entry DSL file, declared dependencies (name → URL), asset list, author, license. This is the 'Cargo.toml' of Artlab. Decide canonical field names, required vs. optional fields, and how to express version constraints.

title: Packages: Browser-side zip reader (parse and extract package contents)
description: Using JSZip (or equivalent zero-dep implementation), implement a PackageReader class that: accepts a File, Blob, or ArrayBuffer; parses the zip; reads artlab.json manifest; provides async methods to read DSL source files and asset blobs by path.

title: Packages: Zip-based package format with static linking and dev-mode URL deps
description: Define and implement the Artlab package format (.zip containing DSL files + assets + manifest). Dev mode loads deps by URL; packaging time fetches and embeds all deps ('static linking'). Upgrading re-dereferences URLs from a pinned package.

title: DSL: Friendly error reporting with source location and hints
description: Implement an error collector that accumulates parse/semantic errors with: file path, line, column, highlighted source snippet, plain-English message, and a 'did you mean?' suggestion where applicable. Error output must be usable both in the IDE console and in terminal.

title: DSL: Import / use statement and library resolution protocol
description: Implement 'use artlab/geometry' and 'use url:https://...' import statements. In dev mode, URL imports are fetched live. In package mode, the static linker has already embedded them. Implement the resolution protocol that the transpiler uses to find library source at compile time.

title: DSL: JavaScript/TypeScript code generator from annotated AST
description: Emit runnable JS/TS from the annotated AST. Output calls to the Artlab runtime API (Renderer, Physics, Scene, Audio). Emit ES module syntax. Include inline source-map comment block so browser DevTools map errors back to DSL line numbers.

title: DSL: Recursive-descent parser producing a typed AST
description: Implement a hand-written recursive-descent parser that consumes the token stream and produces a fully-typed Abstract Syntax Tree. Nodes: Program, ImportDecl, SceneBlock, ObjectDecl, MaterialDecl, PhysicsDecl, AnimationBlock, FunctionDef, ExprStmt, BinaryExpr, CallExpr, MemberExpr, Literal.

title: DSL: Semantic analysis — scope resolution and type inference
description: Walk the AST and perform: symbol table construction, scope resolution (lexical scoping), basic type inference (numeric/string/vec/mat/object), unresolved-reference errors, duplicate-declaration errors, and type-mismatch errors. Annotate the AST with resolved types for the code generator.

title: DSL: Formal grammar specification (EBNF) and syntax design doc
description: Define the complete Artlab DSL grammar in EBNF. Cover: scene blocks, object declarations, physics bindings, material/shader refs, animation timelines, library imports, and composable function definitions. Produce a spec document that drives the parser implementation.

title: DSL: Lexer / tokenizer implementation
description: Implement the tokenizer that converts raw DSL source text into a flat token stream. Handle: keywords, identifiers, numeric literals (int/float/scientific), string literals, operators, delimiters, block comments, line comments, and whitespace skipping. Must preserve source positions for error reporting.

title: DSL: Design and implement the Artlab domain-specific language
description: Artist-forward, composable, human-readable language that transpiles to JS/TS. Inspired by Processing. Covers syntax design, parsing, code generation, and error reporting.

title: Infra: GitHub Actions CI — lint, typecheck, test, build on every PR
description: CI workflow: checkout → pnpm install → eslint → tsc --noEmit → vitest run → vite build. Run on: push to main, all pull requests. Fail fast on any step. Cache pnpm store and Vite build cache. Badge in README.

title: Infra: artlab CLI — create, serve, build, pack, upgrade subcommands
description: Node.js CLI tool (artlab). Subcommands: 'artlab create my-scene' (scaffold new package), 'artlab serve' (dev server with live reload + URL dep resolution), 'artlab build' (transpile to JS), 'artlab pack' (static link + zip), 'artlab upgrade' (re-fetch URL deps + new zip). Installable via npm.

title: Infra: Build tooling, CLI, test framework, CI, documentation
description: Artlab CLI (create/build/pack/serve), Vite build config for the runtime bundle, test suites for DSL transpiler and physics, CI pipeline, and developer documentation site.

title: Demo: Write audio in DSL — proximity pads, FFT bloom binding, background
description: DSL audio block: synth pads assigned per planet (Tone.js notes), proximity-driven volume. Bind bloom.strength to fft.bass reactively. Optional background ambient track. All audio declared in DSL using artlab/audio library.

title: Demo: Build and validate solar-system.zip — load in IDE, verify renders
description: Run the static linker on the solar system package. Verify the zip contains: artlab.json, solar-system.art, all DSL stdlib files, all texture assets. Load in the Artlab IDE. Verify scene renders identically to the current baked implementation. Screenshot for regression baseline.

title: Demo: Write solar-system.art — Sun, 8 planets, Moon, asteroid belt in DSL
description: Main scene DSL file. Define Sun (emissive sphere, corona shader ref, point light). Define each planet using artlab/geometry sphere + artlab/physics/orbital orbit block. Define Moon orbiting Earth. Define asteroid belt (instanced geometry). All planet parameters (radius, orbital elements, texture refs) from PLANET_DATA, expressed in DSL.

title: Demo: Write camera journey in DSL (55-second guided tour via artlab/animation)
description: Define the 4-phase cinematic camera journey in DSL using artlab/cameras path camera and artlab/animation timeline. Phase 1: Earth orbit. Phase 2: inner system pullback. Phase 3: full system wide shot. Phase 4: ecliptic tilt. 'Begin Journey' button wires to the DSL action.

title: Demo: Write solar-system artlab.json manifest with stdlib deps declared
description: Create artlab.json for the solar-system package. Declare: name, version, entry: 'solar-system.art', deps (artlab/physics/orbital, artlab/cameras, artlab/animation, artlab/audio, artlab/ui, artlab/geometry, artlab/lights), asset list, author.

title: Demo: Refactor solar system to a proper Artlab DSL package
description: Convert the current monolithic solar-system JS into a proper Artlab package: artlab.json manifest, DSL scene definition, DSL planet/sun objects, orbital physics via artlab/physics/orbital stdlib, audio via artlab/audio, camera journey via artlab/cameras + artlab/animation, all assets in package zip.

title: StdLib: artlab/ui — CSS2D labels, HUD overlay, progress meters, tooltips
description: DSL UI library: label { text: planet.name, node: planet, style: monospace, color: #aaddff, fade-by-distance: true }. hud { position: top-left, items: [cam-distance, fps, custom-text] }. meter { value: fft.bass, position: bottom-right, style: bar }. Renders via CSS2DRenderer or fixed-position DOM.

title: StdLib: artlab/physics/particles — emitter, force fields, lifetime DSL wrappers
description: DSL particle library: emitter { rate: 100/s, shape: cone(30deg), speed: 5.0, lifetime: 2.0s, color: gradient(#ff4400, #000000), size: curve(1.0, 0.0) }. Forces: gravity, attractor, vortex, wind. Audio-reactive: bind emitter.rate to fft.bass.

title: StdLib: artlab/physics/rigid — rigid body, joint, material DSL wrappers
description: DSL rigid body library: 'use artlab/physics/rigid'. body { shape: sphere(1.0), mass: 5.0, restitution: 0.7, friction: 0.3 }. joint hinge { ... }. Collision event DSL bindings: 'on collision(earth, asteroid) { ... }'. Maps to Rapier.js bodies under the hood.

title: IDE: Package manager pane — add/remove URL deps, pin, upgrade, static link
description: Package manager panel: shows all declared URL dependencies (from artlab.json). Per-dep: URL, current pinned version (hash/tag), 'Upgrade' button (runs PackageUpgrader), 'Remove' button. 'Export / Static Link' button: runs StaticLinker and triggers download of the fully-pinned zip. Shows linker progress.

title: IDE: Error console and output panel — DSL compile errors, runtime logs, warnings
description: Console panel: shows DSL compile errors (with source location links that jump the editor to the offending line), runtime console.log output from the running scene, physics warnings (bodies tunneling, constraint explosions), and renderer warnings (shader compile failures). Filterable by severity. Clearable.

title: IDE: Asset browser — preview textures, 3D models, audio clips in-package
description: Asset browser panel: lists all non-DSL files in the package (images, models, audio). Texture preview: thumbnail grid. Model preview: embedded mini-Three.js viewer. Audio preview: play button + waveform visualization. Drag asset path into editor to insert the correct DSL path string literal.

title: IDE: File/package browser — load zip, create/rename/delete DSL files
description: File tree panel: drag-and-drop a .zip to open a package, or start a new empty package. Create, rename, delete DSL files and asset folders. Double-click to open in editor. Shows unsaved indicator. 'Save Package' button re-serializes all files to a new zip and downloads it.

title: IDE: Code editor — Monaco with DSL syntax grammar, error squiggles, autocomplete
description: Load Monaco Editor (CDN or bundled). Register Artlab DSL language: TextMate grammar for syntax highlighting, monarch tokenizer for fast coloring. Wire error reporter output to Monaco model markers (squiggly underlines). Basic autocomplete: built-in keywords, stdlib names, declared scene symbols. Tab management for multi-file packages.

title: IDE: Live preview pane — embedded Artlab runtime, auto-recompile on save
description: Preview pane: WebGL canvas inside the IDE. On Ctrl+S or after debounced save, re-transpile the active DSL file, hot-swap the scene (dispose old scene, load new one). Show compile errors in console before attempting to load. FPS counter overlay. Camera reset button.

title: IDE: Browser-based Artlab IDE — editor, live preview, package manager, export
description: A single-page-app IDE written in JavaScript. Monaco editor with DSL language support. Embedded live preview. File browser, error console, asset browser, physics debugger, perf monitor, and package export. Runs entirely in-browser, no server needed.

title: IDE: Shell — resizable pane layout (editor | preview | panel stack)
description: IDE shell: three-column resizable layout using CSS grid + drag handles. Left: file/package browser. Center: Monaco editor + tabbed open files. Right: tabbed panels (preview, console, assets, physics, perf). Toolbar: run, stop, open package, save package, export. Dark theme matching Artlab aesthetic. No framework — vanilla JS + CSS.

title: Audio: Synthesizer / pad system — Tone.js-based ambient pads, proximity-driven
description: SynthPads: wraps Tone.js PolySynth. Each scene object (planet, etc.) has an associated pad note/octave. Pad volume driven by camera proximity (distance → gain mapping). Supports: chord voicings, reverb/delay send, filter cutoff modulated by FFT bass. Initialized lazily on user gesture.

title: Audio: Spatial audio — PannerNode positions tied to scene node world positions
description: SpatialAudioSystem: for each audio source component on a scene node, create a PannerNode with HRTF panning. Update panner position/orientation each frame from scene node world transform. Listener position tracks the active camera. Configurable rolloff model (inverse, linear, exponential) and reference distance.

title: Assets: 3D model loader — GLTF 2.0 and OBJ from package zip
description: ModelLoader: extracts GLTF/GLB or OBJ+MTL from package zip, feeds binary to Three.js GLTFLoader/OBJLoader in-memory (no URL needed). Returns a SceneNode subtree. Handles embedded textures (GLTF) or sidecar texture paths (OBJ) resolved via AssetManager.

title: Physics: Simplified SPH fluid simulation (CPU, small-to-medium particle counts)
description: FluidWorld: SPH (Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics) simulation for art-scale fluid effects. Support: pressure, viscosity, surface tension, boundary conditions. Spatial hashing neighbor search. Render as metaballs (marching cubes on a voxel grid, LOD-controlled) or instanced spheres. Target: up to 20k particles at 60 FPS on modern hardware.

title: Physics: Debug renderer — wireframe overlays for all physics body types
description: PhysicsDebugRenderer: subscribes to physics world state each frame and renders: AABB boxes, collision shapes (wireframe), velocity vectors, constraint lines, orbit ellipses, particle trails. Toggled by a keyboard shortcut (default P) or IDE physics debugger pane. Implemented as an extra Three.js scene rendered on top.

title: Renderer: WebGPU backend using renderer.setAnimationLoop and renderAsync
description: WebGPURenderer implementing IRenderer. Use Three.js WebGPURenderer with renderer.setAnimationLoop() and await renderer.renderAsync() for proper async frame submission. TSL-based bloom via three/webgpu PostProcessing. Falls back gracefully to WebGL2 backend if WebGPU is unavailable.

title: Packages: Package upgrader — re-dereference pinned deps to latest URL versions
description: PackageUpgrader: reads a pinned zip package; for each embedded dep, checks the original URL for a newer version (using version manifest or HTTP headers); if newer, fetches and replaces; runs the static linker again; outputs an upgraded zip. This is how versioning/updates work.

title: DSL: Browser REPL / playground (compile + run in-page)
description: Minimal single-page playground: text area for DSL source, a run button, and an embedded Artlab canvas output panel. Useful for testing the transpiler end-to-end without the full IDE. Transpile in a Web Worker to avoid blocking the UI.

title: Implement WebGPU-native post-processing with bloom for Sun/corona
Download and install planet texture assets from Solar System Scope
description:
The scene currently uses procedural canvas-texture fallbacks for all planets. Download 4K-8K textures from https://www.solarsystemscope.com/textures/ and place them in public/textures/.

Required files:
  public/textures/earth/earth_daymap.jpg
  public/textures/earth/earth_nightmap.jpg
  public/textures/earth/earth_clouds.jpg
  public/textures/earth/earth_specular.jpg
  public/textures/earth/earth_normal.jpg
  public/textures/mercury/mercury_map.jpg
  public/textures/venus/venus_atmosphere.jpg
  public/textures/mars/mars_map.jpg
  public/textures/jupiter/jupiter_map.jpg
  public/textures/saturn/saturn_map.jpg
  public/textures/saturn/saturn_rings.png
  public/textures/uranus/uranus_map.jpg
  public/textures/neptune/neptune_map.jpg
  public/textures/moon/moon_map.jpg

TextureManager (src/loaders/TextureLoader.js) hot-swaps real textures over procedural fallbacks automatically once files are present — no code changes needed. public/textures/ is gitignored.

title: Infra: Documentation site — DSL language reference, runtime API, tutorial
description: VitePress or Astro docs site. Content: DSL language reference (every keyword, block type, expression form, with examples), runtime API reference (IRenderer, IPhysicsWorld, EventBus, AssetManager), getting-started tutorial (build a spinning textured sphere from scratch), and the solar system demo walkthrough.

title: IDE: Performance monitor — FPS, draw calls, triangle count, physics step time
description: Perf panel: live line graph of FPS (last 120 frames). Metrics per frame: draw calls, triangle count, visible instances, physics step time (ms), JS frame time (ms), GPU frame time (ms, via EXT_disjoint_timer_query). Toggled from toolbar. Low-FPS threshold warning in the preview pane overlay.

title: IDE: Physics debugger pane — toggle debug renderer, inspect body properties
description: Physics debugger panel: 'Show Physics' toggle (enables PhysicsDebugRenderer overlay on preview canvas). Body list (scrollable): click a body to highlight it and show its properties (mass, velocity, angular velocity, force, energy). Useful for tuning orbital parameters and rigid body setups.

title: Add persistent 3D planet name labels via CSS2DRenderer
description:
Currently planet names appear only in the #planet-label HUD during GSAP camera transitions. No persistent labels float near planets so users can identify them.

Implement CSS2DObject labels:
  - Import CSS2DRenderer, CSS2DObject from three/addons/renderers/CSS2DRenderer.js
  - One label per planet, offset slightly above sphere center
  - Fade by camera distance: full opacity at dist > 2x radius, invisible at < 0.5x
  - Match HUD aesthetic: monospace, color rgba(170,220,255,0.7), letter-spacing 0.2em
  - CSS2DRenderer auto-billboards labels (always face camera)
  - Resize CSS2DRenderer alongside WebGL/WebGPU renderer in src/main.js:168 resize handler

title: Orbit lines: draw ecliptic path for each planet
description:
No orbit path visualization exists. Add thin elliptical lines showing each planet's orbital path around the Sun.

Approach:
  - Use THREE.EllipseCurve (or compute points from keplerPosition at N steps) per planet
  - Render as THREE.Line with LineBasicMaterial, opacity ~0.15, color matching planet tint
  - Group all orbit lines under a single parent Object3D so they can be toggled
  - Add keyboard shortcut O to toggle visibility (document in HUD or tooltip)
